title: Safety Procedures (PD)
description: Fire alarms, first aid, dangerous occurrences, and firefighting procedures.

questions.prompt: Arrange the steps to be taken if the fire alarm sounds whilst you are using a machine into the correct chronological order.

steps.text: Leave the building calmly via the nearest safe exit
Report to your designated assembly point

questions.exampleContext: Imagine the alarm sounds while you are operating a lathe. What is the very first thing you must do to the machine to leave it safe before you calmly evacuate?

questions.prompt: Explain why it is important to use appropriate fire escape routes and report to your assembly point.
questions.exampleContext: If the building is evacuated, emergency services need to know if anyone is trapped inside. Why is it vital that you report to the assembly point immediately?

questions.prompt: Describe the first aid facilities within your specified engineering area.
questions.exampleContext: A colleague has accidentally splashed a mild irritant in their eye. Why is it critical that you already know the location of the nearest eye wash station and first aid kit?

questions.prompt: State at least three dangerous occurrences or malfunctions which should be reported.
questions.exampleContext: You notice a safety guard on a guillotine is completely broken. Why must dangerous occurrences be reported immediately rather than waiting for the end of your shift?

questions.prompt: Summarise what action should be taken on discovering a fire.
questions.exampleContext: You are the first to spot a small fire in a waste bin. What is the very first action you must take before you even consider tackling the fire?

questions.prompt: Summarise the policy regarding fire extinguishers and fire blankets.
questions.exampleContext: You see a fire, but you have never used an extinguisher. Under what strict conditions should you actually attempt to tackle a fire yourself?

questions.prompt: What are the most common causes of fire in a workshop environment?
questions.exampleContext: Think about the combination of heat, fuel, and oxygen in a workshop. What are the most common everyday hazards that can spark a fire if not managed?

questions.prompt: What can you do to prevent fire from occurring?
questions.exampleContext: A cluttered workspace is a dangerous workspace. What daily habits can you adopt to ensure your work area does not become a fire hazard?
